myths.Agent_1:
Before the dawn of mortal life, the Moon was not a sphere of pale stone, but a flawless shield of polished silver forged by the Night Mother. It was so perfectly smooth that the Sun, vain and burning with fiery pride, would spend the twilight hours gazing at her own radiant reflection. The lesser stars, however, grew bitterly jealous of the Sun’s vanity. They were tiny, scattered things, ignored by the daylight and outshone by the Moon’s brilliant mirror at night.

One particular star, a trickster named Vesper, decided to ruin the Sun's favorite vanity glass. Gathering handfuls of heavy cosmic dust and jagged meteoric rock, Vesper hurled the dark debris across the celestial sea. The rocks struck the silver shield with a deafening chime that sent ripples of sound through the endless cosmos. Though the divine silver did not shatter, it dented deeply.

When the Sun next looked to the Moon, her reflection was warped, broken by deep shadows and grey craters. Furious, she chased Vesper away to the very edges of the morning. Yet the damage was permanent. To this day, the Moon wears its scars, a beautifully bruised pearl in the dark.
myths.Agent_2:
Long ago, before the mountains had voices, they were silent giants of stone. They watched the sky, envious of the howling wind and the roaring thunder. Among them was Oros, the tallest peak, who longed to sing like the rivers below.

One day, the Spirit of Sound, a fleeting creature made of pure breath and vibration, swept through the valleys. She was exhausted, having fled a terrible silence that threatened to consume the world. Seeing her plight, Oros offered his deep caverns as a sanctuary. Grateful, the Spirit rested within his stony heart.

When the silence passed, the Spirit prepared to leave. To thank Oros, she bestowed upon him a gift. "You shall never be voiceless again," she whispered. "Whatever is spoken to you, you shall answer back."

From that day on, whenever a traveler called out to the mountains, Oros and his brethren would reply, their voices booming across the valleys. They could not create their own words, but they cherished the borrowed songs of men, beasts, and storms. Thus, the echo was born, an eternal pact between stone and sound, ensuring the mountains would never be silent again. Even today, if you listen closely, you hear his gratitude.

Having been gifted the power of the echo by the Spirit of Sound, Oros grew intensely vain. He wanted to be the only voice the world could hear, drowning out the gentle rustle of the stars and the whispers of the clouds.

The Sky, weary of the mountain’s endless boasting, grew bitter and jealous of the unrelenting noise. She summoned her sharpest weapon—a brilliant spear of lightning—and hurled it down upon Oros's flawless summit. The violent strike shattered the smooth dome, cleaving the solid stone into sharp, jagged peaks and deep, fractured valleys. 

The mountain’s booming voice was broken, splintered into a thousand fragments. When Oros tried to shout, his voice bounced wildly among the ruins, weakening with each bounce. Thus, the jagged peaks were formed, and the proud, deafening shout of the mountain was humbled into the fading, repeating whisper we now know as the echo, forever bouncing through the broken valleys.

Furious that his fiery prey had escaped his freezing jaws, the Wolf tilted his massive head back and unleashed a howl of profound, bitter frustration.

His roar carried the absolute zero of the deepest frost. As the sound waves tore upward through the atmosphere, they collided with the lingering warmth of the Foxes’ celestial trail. The sudden, violent clash of the Wolf's freezing breath and the lingering volcanic friction shattered the night's moisture into millions of intricate, crystalline shards. 

These were the first snowflakes—each one a perfect, frozen echo of the Wolf's furious howl, marked by a tiny, trapped spark of the Foxes' stolen magic. They drifted back down to the earth, blanketing the world in a pristine, silent white. 

Today, whenever the northern skies dance with color and the snow begins to fall, it is the ancient hunt continuing. The delicate snow crystals are the Great Wolf’s icy breath, forever reaching up to capture the untouchable fire of the heavens, freezing the world below.
